data.title: Father Convicted in Connection with Georgia School Shooting
data.description: A court in Georgia has found a father guilty of multiple charges related to a school shooting incident that occurred two years ago. According to Ming Pao, the shooting, carried out by a 14-year-old at a high school, resulted in four deaths and nine injuries. The father was accused of neglecting warnings about his son's threats to attack the school and providing him with a rifle. On Tuesday, he was convicted on 27 counts, including two counts of second-degree murder, and faces a potential sentence of up to 180 years. This marks the first time in the United States that a parent has been convicted of murder in connection with a child's deadly shooting incident.

data.title: Japan Initiates Investigation for Nuclear Waste Disposal on Remote Island
data.description:
Japan's Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry has initiated the first phase of an investigation to assess the suitability of Minamitorishima, a remote Pacific island, for nuclear waste disposal. According to Ming Pao, the island, located 2,000 kilometers from Tokyo and surrounded by coral reefs, is uninhabited and restricted from tourism, with only personnel from the Maritime Self-Defense Force and the Meteorological Agency stationed there.

The investigation process involves three stages before the government can confirm the site for nuclear waste disposal. The first stage, known as the 'documentary survey,' involves studying geological and volcanic activity through existing literature. This will be followed by an 'overview survey,' which includes on-site drilling and excavation. The final stage, the 'detailed survey,' will use underground facilities to examine rock formations and groundwater. The entire investigation is expected to take approximately 20 years.

Previously, similar surveys have been conducted in Suttsu Town and Kamoenai Village in Hokkaido, as well as a location in Saga Prefecture in northwestern Kyushu.

data.title: Bitcoin Miners Quietly Accelerate Liquidation Amid Market Shifts
data.description:
A significant shift is occurring among Bitcoin miners, who collectively manage assets exceeding $8 billion. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that these miners are increasingly liquidating their holdings. This trend is unfolding quietly but with growing momentum, reflecting broader market dynamics.

The move comes as miners face mounting pressures, including fluctuating Bitcoin prices and rising operational costs. These factors are prompting miners to offload their Bitcoin reserves to maintain financial stability and operational viability.

Industry analysts suggest that this liquidation trend could impact Bitcoin's market dynamics, potentially influencing its price and investor sentiment. As miners continue to adjust their strategies in response to economic pressures, the cryptocurrency market may experience further volatility.

This development underscores the challenges faced by Bitcoin miners in navigating the complex and rapidly changing landscape of cryptocurrency markets. As they adapt to these conditions, the broader implications for the market remain to be seen.

data.title: Iran to Intensify Military Actions in Coming Days
data.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard has announced plans to escalate the intensity and scope of its military operations in the coming days. According to Jin10, this statement was reported by Nour News, a media outlet associated with the Iranian government. The announcement comes amid heightened tensions in the region, with Iran signaling a more aggressive stance in its military strategy. The Revolutionary Guard's declaration suggests a potential increase in regional instability as Iran prepares to expand its military activities.

data.title: GMX mounts rally as projects reevaluate effectiveness of buybacks
data.description:
The native token of GMX, the decentralized perpetuals exchange built on Arbitrum, is in the middle of a rally after its governing body approved an overhaul of its buyback strategy over “limited effectiveness. ” The overhaul follows a public admission that its two years of token buybacks have done little to move the market. data.title: U.S. House Committee Approves Subpoena for Attorney General Bondi in Epstein Case Investigation
data.description: The U.S. House of Representatives committee has approved a subpoena for Attorney General Pam Bondi as part of the ongoing investigation into the Epstein case. According to Jin10, the committee aims to gather more information regarding the handling of the case and any potential misconduct. The investigation seeks to understand the circumstances surrounding the case and the actions taken by the Justice Department. This development marks a significant step in the inquiry, as lawmakers continue to scrutinize the details of the Epstein case and the involvement of various officials. The committee's decision reflects the growing demand for transparency and accountability in the matter.

data.title: Nvidia CEO Predicts Growth in Software Stocks Amid AI Advancements
data.description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has expressed confidence in the growth potential of software stocks, highlighting that agentic AI will complement rather than replace current enterprise software. According to NS3.AI, following Nvidia's impressive earnings report, analysts from leading firms have increased their stock targets, indicating robust fundamentals. Although some macroeconomic uncertainties are contributing to short-term market volatility, the general outlook for Nvidia's stock remains positive.

data.title: Trump Administration Explores Insurance Solutions for Hormuz Strait Shipping
data.description: The Trump administration is in discussions with a major insurance brokerage firm to facilitate the passage of ships through the Hormuz Strait. According to Jin10, Marsh Risk Group's team met with government officials on Tuesday, expressing willingness to assist in establishing an insurance mechanism to reduce shipping risks and make vessel insurance more affordable. Marcus Baker, the company's global head of marine business, noted that energy prices have surged and oil transport has stalled since Iran's warning of potential attacks on ships in this strategic waterway. Baker stated, "We have proposed several suggestions on measures they can take, which will be carefully considered."

data.title: Israel Eases Restrictions Amid Reduced Missile Threat from Iran
data.description: The Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) Civil Defense Command announced a relaxation of public restrictions following a slowdown in Iran's ballistic missile launches against Israel. According to Jin10, the latest assessment led to a decision to adjust national activities from 'necessary activities' to 'limited activities' starting at noon local time on March 5. Educational activities remain prohibited, while some businesses are allowed to resume operations from Thursday. These guidelines will remain in effect until Saturday evening, when another evaluation will be conducted.

data.title: Russia Faces Threats Over Oil Supplies Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description:
Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov stated that Slovakia and Hungary, as buyers of Russian oil, are facing threats from Ukraine. According to RTHK, Russian President Vladimir Putin met with Hungarian Foreign Minister Péter Szijjártó on Wednesday at the Kremlin to discuss the impact of disrupted oil supplies due to Ukraine. Peskov also noted that despite the worsening situation in the Middle East, European Union countries have not requested an increase in energy supplies from Russia.

Norwegian Energy Minister Terje Aasland mentioned that the EU has consistently expressed its desire to reduce dependence on Russian oil and gas. However, recent events over the past three to four days have complicated the situation. Given the current geopolitical climate, Aasland believes that discussions on this matter may resume.

Reports from CCTV indicate that Aasland was referring to the U.S. and Israel's strikes on Iran and Iran's subsequent retaliation, which could prompt the EU to revisit discussions on the Russian gas import ban. The situation in the Middle East is reportedly affecting gas exports from the Gulf region, leading to a surge in European gas prices this week.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Praises Google
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed positive sentiments about Google, describing it as a good company. According to Jin10, this statement comes amidst ongoing discussions about the role of major tech companies in the global economy. President Trump's remarks highlight the significance of technology firms in shaping economic landscapes and influencing market dynamics. The acknowledgment of Google's contributions reflects the broader recognition of tech giants' impact on innovation and growth. This development underscores the importance of maintaining a balanced perspective on the influence of technology companies in today's interconnected world.

data.title: U.S. Engages with Insurance Brokers to Address Oil Tanker Blockade
data.description: The United States is actively engaging with insurance brokers to discuss strategies for lifting the blockade on oil tankers. According to Jin10, this move comes amid ongoing efforts to ensure the smooth flow of oil shipments, which are crucial for maintaining global energy stability. The discussions are part of a broader initiative to address the challenges posed by the blockade and to explore potential solutions that could mitigate its impact on the oil market. The outcome of these talks could have significant implications for international trade and energy security.

data.title: Intel Faces Scrutiny Over Ties with Blacklisted ACM Research
data.description: A bipartisan group of six U.S. senators has requested Intel to provide further details regarding its association with ACM Research, a semiconductor equipment manufacturer whose subsidiaries are on the Commerce Department's blacklist. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the senators' concerns about potential national security risks associated with Intel's dealings with ACM Research. The senators emphasized the importance of transparency in business operations that could impact U.S. interests. Intel has yet to respond to the inquiry, which underscores the ongoing scrutiny of tech companies' international partnerships amid geopolitical tensions.

data.title: U.S. Senators Pressure AI Companies Over Surveillance Policies
data.description: U.S. Senators have raised concerns with artificial intelligence companies regarding their surveillance policies. According to Jin10, the lawmakers are scrutinizing how these companies handle data privacy and the potential implications for civil liberties. The move highlights growing apprehension about the role of AI in monitoring and data collection, urging companies to ensure transparency and accountability in their operations. This development reflects a broader trend of increased regulatory focus on technology firms and their impact on society.

data.title: Silicon Valley Faces Challenges Amid DeepSeek Release
data.description: Silicon Valley is experiencing internal conflicts over safety concerns and accusations of copying as it prepares for the upcoming release of DeepSeek. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the industry's struggles to advance in the competitive tech landscape. These issues are seen as detrimental to progress, potentially hindering innovation and collaboration within the sector. As companies navigate these challenges, the focus remains on maintaining a balance between competition and cooperation to ensure future growth and development.

data.title: U.S. Military Focus in Middle East Crucial for Change in Iran, Says Blinken
data.description: Former Secretary of State Antony Blinken has emphasized that genuine change in Iran necessitates a continued U.S. military presence in the Middle East. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Blinken's concerns that this focus could potentially expose the United States to threats from other global powers, such as Russia and China. Blinken's remarks underscore the complexities of balancing regional stability with broader geopolitical risks.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump: We Will Continue to Move Forward
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed his commitment to progress, emphasizing the importance of moving forward. According to Jin10, Trump's statement reflects his administration's focus on advancing policies and initiatives. The President's remarks come amid various challenges and opportunities facing the nation, highlighting his determination to lead the country towards growth and development. Trump's leadership continues to shape the political landscape, influencing both domestic and international affairs.

data.title: Ukraine's Zelensky Engages in Talks with Bahrain and Kuwait on Iran Issue
data.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has engaged in discussions with leaders from Bahrain and Kuwait regarding the situation with Iran. According to Jin10, Zelensky emphasized that once the security situation around Iran stabilizes, trilateral diplomatic efforts to address the conflict with Russia will resume.

data.title: STOCKS | Investors Increase Short Positions in Blue Owl Amid Decline
data.description: Investors are increasing their short positions in Blue Owl Capital, anticipating further declines in the stock's value. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the stock has already lost nearly one-third of its value this year. Despite this significant drop, market participants believe there is still potential for additional downward movement. Blue Owl, known for its asset management services, has faced challenges in maintaining its stock performance, prompting investors to bet against its recovery. The trend reflects broader market sentiments and strategic positioning by investors seeking to capitalize on perceived weaknesses in Blue Owl's financial outlook.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Praises Venezuelan President's Performance
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed commendation for Venezuelan President Delcy Rodríguez, stating that she is doing an excellent job. According to Jin10, Trump's remarks come amid ongoing discussions about Venezuela's political and economic situation. The U.S. administration has been closely monitoring developments in the region, particularly in light of Venezuela's challenges and international relations. Trump's comments highlight the diplomatic interactions between the two nations and the U.S. perspective on Venezuela's leadership.
